title: Check addition overflow using lt(after, before) instead of checked arithmetic
description: When two uint256 values are added and the result overflows, it wraps around to a smaller value. Checking lt(result, operand) detects this in a single opcode, cheaper than Solidity's built-in overflow detection which compiles to a more expensive sequence.
pattern_before:
// Solidity checked math — compiler generates extra opcodes
totalSupply += amount;
when_to_apply: Any uint256 addition where overflow is possible and must be caught
when_not_to_apply: Additions that are provably safe (e.g. sum of values already bounded by totalSupply)
